visions.style: Aida’s POV fills the entire frame with the vision; only the wooden pier, waiting sailboat, bright water, Keen, and Mum are seen inside rose-pink haze at the margins, with no sanctuary visible around it.
visions.materialize: Rose-quartz light coalesces from soft darkness inside the full-frame vision, drifting into the later pier image before dissolving back to Aida.

recurring.name: Fuzzby and Zenny screen relationship
recurring.appearance: Both use their locked turnarounds unchanged; Fuzzby remains the larger male bee, Zenny the smaller female bee, with no added body props or accessories.
recurring.orientation: When they share frame, Fuzzby holds frame-left energy and Zenny holds frame-right deadpan counterbalance unless action requires a brief pass-through.

recurring.name: Squeaky
recurring.appearance: Locked dolphin model sheet unchanged throughout; no added markings or accessories.
recurring.orientation: Introduced beside Keen’s boat, usually screen-right of the boat as guide and rescue partner, then exits back toward open water.

recurring.name: Keen's Mum
recurring.appearance: Locked Keen's Mum reference unchanged; no added clothing or accessories beyond canon reference.
recurring.orientation: Holds the pier side as Keen moves away from her toward open water, remaining the emotional home anchor behind him.

persistent.in: On Keen from the pier goodbye through the storm, rescue, arrival, and pre-award gathering.
On Keen from the Aquamarine gift onward through the final beach walk.
Packed into the sailboat at departure; their careful arrangement establishes his need to look ready before the storm strips control away.
Across the storm sea from Crystal Cove toward Keen, guiding the emotional geography of Scenes 6 and 7.

lost.name: Keen's map
lost.reason: The storm gust snatches it from his paws, leaving him physically lost and emotionally exposed.

lost.name: Keen's sailboat
lost.reason: The storm overtakes the rescue sequence; by the shore arrival it is gone, proving Keen cannot return to control or equipment for safety.

lost.name: Ragged drift net
lost.reason: Keen tears it free from Squeaky’s tail; its threat is ended in the same wave impact that throws Keen sideways.

items.name: Father's cuffs — vacant
items.appearance: Gold cuffs with empty kite-shaped recesses, carried as inherited love before any Aquamarine gems appear.

items.name: Father's cuffs — crystal-set
items.appearance: Gold cuffs now holding faceted Aquamarine kite gems, glowing azure after the award.

items.name: Aida's crystal bowl
items.appearance: Sanctuary bowl used for the low shimmering tone that opens Aida’s vision; remains in the sanctuary only.

items.name: Keen's map
items.appearance: Rolled map loaded carefully, then unfolded at sea and lost to the storm.

items.name: Small sailboat
items.appearance: Neat little sailboat at the pier and on open water; later absent after the storm rescue.

items.name: Ragged drift net
items.appearance: Tangled underwater net wrapped around Squeaky’s tail until Keen tears it free.

items.name: Aquamarine crystal
items.appearance: A glowing aquamarine light-form that appears between Aida and Keen, splits into two gems, and settles into the cuffs.

worldState.change: Warm pollen meadow shifts from playful breeze to a distant thunder warning.
Aida’s rose-quartz vision establishes the pier goodbye as emotionally connected to Crystal Cove before Keen arrives.
Keen leaves home; Mum remains behind on the dock as the boat moves away, making separation irreversible for the episode.
Open blue water becomes storm water; map is lost and the route becomes unknowable.
The bees discover the tiny boat in danger through rain, turning the comic thread toward rescue urgency.
The shore becomes the rescue lookout; combined light creates a beacon through the storm.
Underwater danger peaks; Squeaky is freed, Keen’s vacant cuffs self-glow, and the boat is effectively lost.
The same shore shifts from emergency lookout to welcome space as the storm eases and Keen is steadied.
Rain-washed gathering space becomes the place where Keen’s courage is witnessed and his cuffs become crystal-set.
The beach settles into post-storm peace; Keen now walks with the group as someone who belongs.
